name: Pumpkin Spice Cake in Jars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
2. Sterilize 8 (1‑pint, straight-sided) Kerr or Ball Quilted Crystal canning jars, lids, and rings by boiling them in water for 10 minutes. Remove from water and let air-dry. Keep the lids and rings in hot water until ready to use. Once cool, brush the inside of each jar with shortening (do not use Pam or Baker's Secret); set aside.
3. Coarsely chop walnuts and raisins; set aside.
4. In a large bowl, sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, ground cloves, cinnamon, and ginger. Add the chopped raisins and walnuts; mix lightly.
5. In another large bowl, beat eggs at high speed until thick and yellow (2–3 minutes). Gradually beat in sugar until thick and light. Beat in oil and pumpkin; blend thoroughly. Gradually stir in the flour mixture until well combined.
6. Divide batter among the 8 jars so they are slightly less than half full. Wipe any spilled batter from the jar sides inside/outside to prevent burning. Place jars on a cookie sheet or they may tip over during baking. Bake in the preheated oven at 325°F (165°C) for 35–40 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted deep into the center comes out clean.
7. When cakes are done, remove each jar, place a lid and ring on top tightly. Use heavy-duty mitts when handling hot jars! Place jars on a counter to cool. A sealed jar will make a "plinking" sound; if not audible, press the lids once cooled— they should not move at all. These can keep in a cool, dark place for about 1 year.
